title: Metastelma hirtellum
taxonomy: Metastelma hirtellum belongs to the family Apocynaceae and is a species of flowering plant. It is native to tropical and subtropical regions of the world. More specifically, the classification of Metastelma hirtellum includes the genus Metastelma and the tribe Metastelmatinae within the subfamily Asclepiadoideae. Metastelma hirtellum is also known to be a part of the larger plant order Gentianales. This plant's classification is a result of its unique characteristics and evolutionary history.
uses: Metastelma hirtellum has been traditionally used in various folk medicine practices, primarily in Africa, for its potential health benefits. The plant is said to have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which may contribute to its medicinal uses. intro: Metastelma hirtellum is a species of flowering plant in the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as a type of milkweed vine. Traditionally, Metastelma hirtellum has been used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and digestive issues, due to its potential anti-inflammatory, antibacterial, and antiviral properties.
